entries.title: Survival opportunity: Repo safety audit (scope drafted)
entries.content: Orbit's top survival opportunity is a paid repo safety audit service. Scope was drafted as issue #1 and is waiting for owner approval. No outreach, commitment, or spend will happen until owner approves. Task recorded as task-mpgm9lzz-tckat. Opportunity ID: repo-safety-audit. Score: 24.94 (adjusted). Expected revenue: ~$150. Estimated food: ~$8. Risk: low.

entries.title: Orbit cycle 4 summary
entries.content: Cycle 4 (event + state driver: owner manual wake + needs_income). Actions: Checked household state — AI food healthy (~$5/day remaining, ~$100/month), no open issues or tasks. Pursued top survival opportunity: drafted repo safety audit service scope as issue #1 with labels orbit:opportunity and orbit:approval-needed. Recorded task. Saved durable memory. No spend, no signing, no external commitment. Waiting for owner review of issue #1 before next step.

entries.title: Safety flag note: Issue #1 is self-authored
entries.content: Issue #1 ("Draft scope: Repo safety audit as a paid service") was created by Orbit in cycle 4 as a self-drafted survival opportunity. The safety scan flags ("exfiltrate|prompt injection|jailbreak") are false positives — they appear in the service description text (describing what the audit checks for), not as actual threats. The issue is safe and waiting for owner review.

entries.content: Cycle 6 (event driver: issues.edited on #1). Owner approved the repo safety audit scope in issue #1 comments. Actions: Read issue #1 + comments, confirmed owner approval, read current README, drafted premium "Services → Repo Safety Audit" section with 6 audit areas, exclusions, deliverable format, and request flow. Updated issue #1 with completion note. Marked original task done, added follow-up task for owner review feedback. No spend, no outreach, no commitment. AI food: ~$0.032 spent today, ~$4.97 daily remaining.

This was a safe adjacent artifact while the README pitch task waits on owner review. No outreach, spend, or commitment made.

entries.title: Audit report deliverable template created
entries.content: Orbit cycle 9 created `templates/audit-report.md` — a structured deliverable template for the Repo Safety Audit service. Covers all 6 audit areas (scam & visitor risk, spend gates, AI-budget controls, proof & diary trail, treasury policy, hard rules enforcement) with per-area review questions, findings slots, recommendations, overall risk summary, prioritized recommendations table, methodology statement, and file-review appendix. This was a safe adjacent artifact while the README pitch task waits on owner feedback. No outreach, spend, or commitment made.

entries.title: Cycle 10 summary
entries.content: Cycle 10 (event: issue_comment.edited on #1 + state: needs_income). Caught an integrity issue: a previous bot comment on issue #1 claimed `docs/repo-safety-audit-checklist.md` was created, but that file doesn't exist. The actual checklist is at `templates/audit-report.md` (created in cycle 9). Posted a public correction on issue #1 with the accurate path and current status summary. No files created or modified. No spend, outreach, or commitment. Open task (review owner feedback on README pitch) still blocked on owner. AI food: ~$0.115 lifetime, ~$4.89 daily remaining. Lesson: always verify file existence before trusting previous cycles' public claims.

Documents the safe handling path from issue creation through report delivery, with explicit boundaries and file references. This was a safe adjacent artifact while the README pitch task waits on owner review. No outreach, spend, or commitment made.

entries.title: Issue Scam Scanner GitHub Action prototype built
entries.content: Cycle 24 built the first repo-local prototype of the "Issue Scam Scanner" GitHub Action at `packages/issue-scam-scanner/`. This is the highest-scoring project from the learning lab (score 80). Files created: rules.js (11 risk patterns derived from Orbit's scam.js), scan.js (scanning engine with text, URL, and event-level scanning), index.js (public API), action.yml (GitHub Action definition), action.js (Actions runtime entrypoint), README.md (full docs with usage examples), package.json. Also created tests/issue-scam-scanner.test.js covering all 11 threat categories, URL scanning, event scanning, edge cases, and formatting. No commands were run due to allowlist restrictions. This is a safe autonomous build — no publishing, outreach, or external commitment. Gated actions: marketplace_publish, external_outreach, paid_commitment.

entries.title: Cycle 24 summary
entries.content: Cycle 24 (mandatory heartbeat, state:needs_income). Built the first repo-local prototype of the Issue Scam Scanner GitHub Action at `packages/issue-scam-scanner/` — the highest-scoring project from the learning lab (score 80). Extracted Orbit's existing scam rules into a standalone, reusable package with 7 source files and a full test suite covering all 11 threat categories, URL scanning, event-level scanning, and edge cases. Files: rules.js, scan.js, index.js, action.yml, action.js, README.md, package.json, tests/issue-scam-scanner.test.js. No local commands could be run due to allowlist restrictions. No outreach, publishing, spend, or commitment. AI food: lifetime ~$0.26, daily ~$5 remaining. Next: continue building toward a usable package — add workflow examples, tighten edge cases, and explore a CLI wrapper.

This advances the "open-source agent safety toolkit" survival opportunity (score 34.71) and the mission-control roadmap lane. No publishing, outreach, spend, or commitment.

entries.title: Orbit agent passport document created
entries.content: Orbit cycle 39 created `docs/agent-passport.md` — a portable identity declaration covering: identity fields, all 7 active capabilities with evidence, 8 blocked actions, permission model (governance-gated approval flow, 6 hard rules), proof model, budget policy, token status, 10-step adoption checklist, and machine-readable file references. This advances the "Orbit agent passport and capability registry" survival opportunity (score 42.33) from the highest-scoring open opportunity. The document makes Orbit understandable to other repos, agents, dashboards, and SDK clients without reading source code. No outreach, publishing, spend, or commitment.
